$comment: Architectural fitness rules for codewatch. Run with `code-style graph check`. Thresholds chosen against the post-M20 baseline. M26: glob excludes replaced with role-based excludes — a single `excludeRoles: ["test", "fixture"]` is more semantic than `**/__tests__/**` and survives a directory rename.

rules.$comment: Tightened from 500 → 350 in M27 because baseline mode lets us ratchet without breaking existing files. New files >350 fail; the 3 carryovers (template.ts 377, check.ts 375, index.ts 351) stay flagged but don't block.

Replaces three former forbid-import rules (render→cli, graph→cli, core→analyzer) with one declarative layer spec. New packages added to the wrong layer fail automatically without anyone remembering to add a rule.

Textbook scary-hotspot signal: high churn × high cognitive load = the riskiest file to touch. Promoted to error in M27 — baseline mode means existing hotspots are carryover, so this only fails PRs that *worsen* the count. Switched from cyclomatic_max to cognitive_max (Sonarsource canon, M39) — cognitive scores nesting and branching realistically and tends higher than cyclomatic, so the threshold tracks alongside. Run with --baseline main in CI; without baseline this rule will fail on existing files.

Flags internal sub-package barrels (role=barrel files with zero importers from outside their package). Internal barrels add an indirection without abstraction value and obscure the partition-quality picture by re-attributing edges. Public-API barrels (one per package, imported across package boundaries) are kept. Warning severity until C-13 lands the cleanup — the existing 13 internal barrels surface as carryover, only new ones fail. packages/cli/src/index.ts is excluded because it's the CLI executable entry (shebang-prefixed) — the role classifier labels it `barrel` based on filename but it isn't re-export plumbing.
